title: Drip vs Buttondown (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Drip and Buttondown email marketing tools. See pricing, features, and which fits your needs best.
intro: Drip and Buttondown serve different segments of the email marketing market. Drip focuses on e-commerce automation with sophisticated revenue tracking, while Buttondown caters to developers and technical writers seeking simplicity and markdown support. Choosing between them depends on your business type, technical preferences, and budget.
choose_a_if: You run an e-commerce store and need deep integration with shopping platforms
Revenue attribution and order tracking are critical to your marketing strategy
You want SMS capabilities alongside email marketing
choose_b_if: You prefer writing newsletters in markdown and want developer-friendly tools
You're starting out and need a free or low-cost email marketing solution
You want to offer paid subscriptions to your audience

faq.a: Neither is universally better; it depends on your use case. Drip excels for e-commerce businesses needing sophisticated tracking and automation, while Buttondown wins for newsletter writers and developers who value simplicity and affordability. Buttondown has higher customer satisfaction ratings (4.8 vs 4.4 on G2), but Drip offers more enterprise-level features.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Drip or Buttondown?
faq.a: Buttondown is significantly cheaper, starting at free and scaling to just $9/month for paid plans. Drip has no free tier and starts at $39/month. If budget is your primary concern, Buttondown offers exceptional value, especially for growing newsletters under 100 subscribers.

faq.q: Can I switch from Buttondown to Drip?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms support import/export functionality and standard email list formats, making migration possible. However, you may lose some Buttondown-specific features like markdown formatting history. Plan the transition carefully to maintain subscriber data and unsubscribe compliance.
